entity.summary: Turkey-based centralized cryptocurrency exchange that stopped all activities in April 2021 amid financial strain, regulatory pressure, account blocking, and a local investigation.
entity.notes: Death reason is recorded as regulation because the shutdown coincided with Turkish account blocking and a prosecutor-led investigation, though financial strain was also cited.

events.title: Vebitcoin launched
events.description: Vebitcoin operated as a Turkey-based digital asset trading platform founded in August 2017.
events.notes: Month-level launch date based on exchange database reference.

events.title: Activities stopped and investigation began
events.description: Vebitcoin announced that it stopped all activities, while Turkish authorities later blocked onshore bank accounts and detained people linked to the exchange as part of an investigation.
events.notes: Used as death_date because the exchange said it had stopped all activities.

evidence.title: Vebitcoin trade volume and market listings
evidence.notes: Used for founding month, Turkey location, and untracked exchange-market status.

evidence.title: A second bitcoin exchange collapses in Turkey amid crackdown on cryptocurrencies
evidence.notes: Used for the all-activities-stopped claim and financial-strain context.

evidence.title: Turkey detains four in Vebitcoin investigation
evidence.notes: Reuters-distributed report used for account blocking, detentions, and investigation context.

evidence.title: Turkish Authorities Detain Four in Probe of Vebitcoin Crypto Exchange: Report
evidence.notes: Additional report on the investigation and exchange trouble context.
